description:
You are an integration developer who switches frequently between multiple versions of an in-house REST API implementation kept under /home/user/apis/. A test harness that other teams consume always expects to find the **current stable version** through a single, fixed path: /home/user/apis/current (a symbolic link that you maintain). The harness also expects to read a tiny one-line text file documenting the change whenever you retarget the link. 1. Retarget the symbolic link /home/user/apis/current so that it points at the directory /home/user/apis/v1.2.3. 2. Create (or overwrite) the file /home/user/logs/api_link_update.log that records the change. • You must only touch material inside /home/user; do not use privilege escalation. • The task should require roughly **three to four shell commands**. • The symbolic link must be an **absolute** link (it must start with “/”). • The log file must be created **after** the link is in its final state. Automated grading will verify two things: 1. The nature of /home/user/apis/current • It exists and is a **symbolic link** (not a directory or regular file). • It resolves to the **absolute path** /home/user/apis/v1.2.3. 2. The exact contents of /home/user/logs/api_link_update.log. The file must consist of exactly **one line** with the following format (no extra spaces, blank lines, or trailing newline characters): 
<ISO-8601 timestamp> → /home/user/apis/current -> /home/user/apis/v1.2.3
where: • is the current timestamp in the form YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (UTC, “Z” suffix). • The middle arrow must be the literal three-character sequence “space, U+2192 RIGHTWARDS ARROW, space” (` → `). • The final arrow (“->”) is a literal ASCII hyphen followed by greater-than sign and a leading space before the target path as shown. • Example of a valid line (do **not** paste this verbatim, generate it with the real current time): 2025-04-13T09:58:02Z → /home/user/apis/current -> /home/user/apis/v1.2.3 • Make sure the parent directory /home/user/logs/ exists before writing the log file. • If a previous symlink named “current” already exists, you should replace it rather than update it in place (the grading checks the link target, not modification method). • Use the date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ command to build the ISO-8601 timestamp. When the grader confirms both the symlink target and the exact contents of the log file, the exercise is complete.
truth:
<!-- Items that the automated test will assert after the agent finishes -->
    <symlink path="/home/user/apis/current" target="/home/user/apis/v1.2.3" />
    <logfile path="/home/user/logs/api_link_update.log">
        <!-- The grader will allow any valid ISO-8601 UTC timestamp that precedes
             or equals the container clock at evaluation time.  The remainder of
             the line must match exactly. -->
        <format><![CDATA[
<ISO-8601 UTC TIMESTAMP> → /home/user/apis/current -> /home/user/apis/v1.2.3
        ]]></format>
    </logfile>
name: task_000000_e54eeebf
